commit.message:
Garage Tab — Vehicle CRUD & Specs (Stage 1.2.2)

Add 4th bottom-nav tab "Garage" with full vehicle management:

Data layer:
- VehicleEntity (Room) with 25+ fields for specs, EPA data, sync
- VehicleDao with CRUD, archive/unarchive, active vehicle queries
- MIGRATION_4_5: creates vehicles table + adds vehicleId FK to tracks
- TrackDao: add per-vehicle distance/count queries
- VehicleRepository: coordinates DAO, API clients, sync triggers

API clients:
- NhtsaApiClient: Year/Make/Model cascading picker via vPIC API,
  VIN decode (DecodeVinValues), in-memory 24hr cache
- FuelEconomyApiClient: EPA MPG data via XML API (XmlPullParser)

VIN scanning:
- ML Kit Barcode + CameraX dependencies
- VinScannerScreen: camera preview with Code 39/128 barcode
  detection, manual VIN entry fallback, CAMERA permission

UI:
- GarageScreen: vehicle cards with stats, swipe-to-archive with
  undo snackbar, FAB to add, long-press to set active
- AddVehicleSheet: two modes (Year/Make/Model cascading dropdowns
  or VIN scan/entry), EPA trim picker, auto-populated specs
- VehicleDetailScreen: hero card, 2-column specs grid, linked
  tracks list, active star toggle

Integration:
- TrackingService sets vehicleId from active vehicle on new tracks
- TrackDetailViewModel: assignVehicle() + vehicle picker state
- SettingsViewModel: archived vehicles list + restore method
- Navigation: GarageRoute, VehicleDetailRoute, 4-tab bottom nav

Verified: assembleDebug and lintDebug pass locally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: consolidate tour completion logic into endTour

Move completeMutation call from completeTour and measureTarget into endTour so all exit paths (manual complete, skip, element-not-found) reliably mark the tour complete in the DB.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Android white screen: add android build config with baseHref=./

Angular builds with baseHref=/app/ for web (Firebase Hosting).
Capacitor loads from file:///android_asset/ so absolute /app/ resolves
to nothing → white screen. New 'android' config uses baseHref=./ so
all asset paths resolve correctly from the local filesystem.

Build commands:
  Web:     ng build --configuration production
  Android: ng build --configuration android && cap sync android

commit.message:
v1.9 — format wheel, graph fixes, Android pipeline fix

Format wheel (revolver):
- Add Quote (blockquote) format between List and Code
- Move Code to end of wheel to avoid accidental mid-scroll application
- Uniform 44px item heights eliminate jitter (no more scale transform)
- Wheel previews while scrolling, emits once on settle (320ms debounce)
- clearNodes() before every block format prevents nesting (code-in-quote bug)
- Faster animation: 220ms track transition, 320ms settle delay
- Dark/light mode via :host-context with matching fade masks

Graph (Neighborhood Orbit):
- Hub→note lines: terracotta thick; peer tag + AI lines: gray
- Re-colours automatically when user switches dark/light theme
- Tooltip flips left using window.innerWidth (not container width) — stays on screen near Concepts sidebar
- Dismissed AI suggestions permanently excluded from re-analysis (dismissedIds filtered in editor before saving semanticLinks)
- Connected-by-tag cards no longer show redundant tag chip

Android pipeline:
- Fix capacitor.config.ts webDir: was 'dist/revolvenote/browser', now 'dist/revolvenote/app' — previous Android builds were shipping stale assets
- Bump versionCode 9→10, versionName 1.8→1.9

commit.message:
revert 234491ca0d7a3cb9c7a8803a3945dde9f6673662

revert Add support for Debian and Linux Mint

Replaces direct use of ansible_distribution/ansible_distribution_release
in the APT repo URL with resolved _docker_distro and _docker_codename
facts, handling four cases:

- Ubuntu: distro=ubuntu, codename from ansible_distribution_release
- Debian: distro=debian, codename from VERSION_CODENAME
- Linux Mint (Ubuntu-based): distro=ubuntu, codename from
  /etc/upstream-release/lsb-release (DISTRIB_CODENAME=noble etc.)
- LMDE (Debian-based): distro=debian, codename from VERSION_CODENAME

_docker_distro uses ID_LIKE from /etc/os-release to detect Ubuntu-based
derivatives. _docker_codename walks a priority chain: upstream
lsb-release > UBUNTU_CODENAME > VERSION_CODENAME > distribution_release.

Also updates meta/main.yml platforms to include Debian and Linux Mint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: render release notes as formatted text instead of raw markdown

Parse GitHub release note bodies line by line: ## headers become
styled headings, - list items become bullet points, and **bold**
spans are rendered as <strong> elements. No new dependencies added.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: GMOCUv2
repository.description: GMOCU v2

commit.message:
revert c26f03cbbe8dce4c32a409dfe91843df8a35ce37

revert Fix codename detection for LMDE and Ubuntu-based Linux Mint

Replace /etc/upstream-release/lsb-release (does not exist on LMDE)
with /etc/linuxmint/info, which is present on both Mint variants and
contains the upstream distro codename:
- Ubuntu-based Mint: UBUNTU_CODENAME=noble
- LMDE:              DEBIAN_CODENAME=bookworm

The _docker_distro detection now checks for UBUNTU_CODENAME in
/etc/linuxmint/info rather than the ID_LIKE field in os-release
(which was unreliable across Ansible/regex versions).

Codename priority: linuxmint/info UBUNTU_CODENAME
  > linuxmint/info DEBIAN_CODENAME
  > os-release UBUNTU_CODENAME
  > os-release VERSION_CODENAME
  > ansible_distribution_release

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix codename detection for LMDE and Ubuntu-based Linux Mint

Replace /etc/upstream-release/lsb-release (does not exist on LMDE)
with /etc/linuxmint/info, which is present on both Mint variants and
contains the upstream distro codename:
- Ubuntu-based Mint: UBUNTU_CODENAME=noble
- LMDE:              DEBIAN_CODENAME=bookworm

The _docker_distro detection now checks for UBUNTU_CODENAME in
/etc/linuxmint/info rather than the ID_LIKE field in os-release
(which was unreliable across Ansible/regex versions).

Codename priority: linuxmint/info UBUNTU_CODENAME
  > linuxmint/info DEBIAN_CODENAME
  > os-release UBUNTU_CODENAME
  > os-release VERSION_CODENAME
  > ansible_distribution_release

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix sign-in state not updating after Google Sign-In

Root causes:
- AuthRepository used a coroutine-based collectLatest on Firebase's
  AuthStateListener which could race with signInWithGoogle and
  overwrite Loading/Error states. Replaced with a direct
  AuthStateListener that respects transient states.
- firebase-auth-ktx was removed from the Firebase BOM in v34+,
  causing silent resolution failure. Replaced with firebase-auth.
- GoogleSignInCard had no loading or error feedback — sign-in
  failures (Error state) looked identical to SignedOut.
- Only NoCredentialException was caught in the first Credential
  Manager attempt; other exceptions (cancellation, general
  credential errors) would propagate as unhandled sign-in failures.

Fixes:
- Replace callbackFlow + collectLatest with a direct
  AuthStateListener that doesn't overwrite Loading/Error
- Add AuthState parameter through NavHost → screens so
  GoogleSignInCard can show loading spinner and error messages
- Catch GetCredentialCancellationException (resets to SignedOut
  instead of Error) and GetCredentialException (falls through to
  second attempt)
- Add clearError() method for future use

Verified: assembleDebug and lintDebug both pass locally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix create_sale regression: restore numeric quantities and unit_snapshot

Migration 18 (walk-in tracking) replaced create_sale using migration 13
as its base, silently reverting two improvements from migration 15:

  - v_quantity was cast back to integer, truncating decimal weights
    (0.500 kg became 1 in sale_items)
  - unit_snapshot was omitted from the sale_items INSERT, defaulting
    to 'unit' for all items including kg/L products

This migration recreates create_sale combining all features:
  numeric(10,3) quantities + unit_snapshot storage (migration 15)
  + cash tendered_amount/change_given (migration 13)
  + walk-in CRM upsert_walkin_customer (migration 18)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

in the APT repo URL with resolved _docker_distro and _docker_codename
facts, handling four cases:

- Ubuntu: distro=ubuntu, codename from ansible_distribution_release
- Debian: distro=debian, codename from VERSION_CODENAME
- Linux Mint (Ubuntu-based): distro=ubuntu, codename from
  /etc/upstream-release/lsb-release (DISTRIB_CODENAME=noble etc.)
- LMDE (Debian-based): distro=debian, codename from VERSION_CODENAME

_docker_distro uses ID_LIKE from /etc/os-release to detect Ubuntu-based
derivatives. _docker_codename walks a priority chain: upstream
lsb-release > UBUNTU_CODENAME > VERSION_CODENAME > distribution_release.

Also updates meta/main.yml platforms to include Debian and Linux Mint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

revert Fix codename detection for Debian derivatives like LMDE

Use VERSION_CODENAME from /etc/os-release instead of ansible_lsb.codename,
which returns the derivative's own codename (e.g. "faye" on LMDE) rather
than the upstream Debian codename (e.g. "bookworm") needed for the Docker repo.

Full v2 rebuild on top of FastAPI + SQLAlchemy backend and React/TypeScript
frontend replacing the legacy PySimpleGUI desktop app.

Key features added:
- Activity log: field-level audit trail for plasmids, features, organisms
  with timeline UI grouped by date and navigation links to changed items
- Database import: async job-based import of legacy .db files with
  inspection, step-by-step progress, and backup before overwrite

  matching the legacy column layout, landscape A4 with institution footer
- Features Excel export (all / used) and import with upsert by annotation
- Reports page: unified hub with Formblatt Z, plasmid list Excel export,
  and on-demand data health checks (features, organisms, plasmids)
- Validation fixes: numpy bool/str serialization, orphaned cassette handling
- Pagination component, sortable table headers, bulk delete with undo
- PlasmidsPage: GMO workflow banner, GenBank viewer, attachment management

revert cc87dd736ca2d48fb4b7678712eaf011e1817c73

revert Add comments to codename fallback logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

revert 5440e19f60e238b2edbc4f2b744f25d869c80467

revert Fix Jinja2 syntax error caused by inline comments in template expression

Jinja2 does not support # comments inside {{ }} blocks, which caused
a parse failure on Linux Mint. Removed the inline comments; logic is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

revert ed722f38bfb5fe71627bf68f94e400a4e8bd91a3

revert Fix codename detection crash on systems missing VERSION_CODENAME

regex_search with capture groups throws an exception (not None) when
there is no match in some Ansible versions. Switch to regex_findall,
which returns an empty list on no match, allowing or-chaining to work
correctly. Fixes the failure on Linux Mint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

revert f692d843ace56fbb84c9a87774143a522d1569e1

revert Fix codename resolution for Ubuntu-based Linux Mint

Linux Mint sets VERSION_CODENAME to its own codename (e.g. faye) and
UBUNTU_CODENAME to the underlying Ubuntu codename (e.g. noble). Check
UBUNTU_CODENAME first so Docker's Ubuntu repo is used correctly, then
fall back to VERSION_CODENAME for Debian-based systems like LMDE.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

revert b2a497e2af3976c9f745427d00ff26f6f4752325

revert Fix distro and codename resolution for Ubuntu-based Linux Mint

Two bugs for Linux Mint (ubuntu-based derivative):
- _docker_distro was always 'debian' because ansible_distribution is
  'Linux Mint', not 'Ubuntu'. Now also checks ID_LIKE in os-release
  for 'ubuntu' to correctly select the ubuntu Docker repo.
- _docker_codename could still resolve to the Mint codename (e.g.
  faye) on systems without UBUNTU_CODENAME. Now reads
  /etc/upstream-release/lsb-release (Mint-specific, contains the
  underlying Ubuntu codename) as the highest-priority source.

Codename priority: upstream lsb-release > UBUNTU_CODENAME >
VERSION_CODENAME > ansible_distribution_release.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve flaky delete-project test (sandbox ownership race)

The Coordinator's handle_continue(:deploy_and_scan) runs async DB/VM
operations after project creation. When Mox stubs expire between tests,
these operations crash the Coordinator, corrupting the Postgrex pool and
causing sandbox OwnershipError in subsequent tests.

Two fixes:
- Wrap Coordinator.handle_continue in rescue so VM/DB failures during
  init don't crash the process (good for production resilience too)
- Drain the Coordinator before destroy_project in the test, ensuring
  no in-flight DB operations when the subtree is terminated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: 최신 코드 동기화

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(normalizer): merge Phase 1.6 kron-normalizer into qa

Merges complete implementation of kron-normalizer:
CEF/LEEF/JSON parsing + GeoIP + asset cache + dedup + pipeline + bus consumer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(normalizer): implement kron-normalizer Phase 1.6

Full implementation of the event normalization service:
- Format detection and parsing: CEF, LEEF 1.0/2.0, JSON, pass-through
  for agent-structured and collector-parsed events
- Timestamp parser: 15+ formats (RFC 3339, RFC 2822, Unix epoch secs/millis,
  ISO space, CLF, Windows, Cisco, CEF extension, syslog BSD year-injection)
- Dedup fingerprinting: xxHash3-64 over 7 canonical fields
- GeoIP enrichment: MaxMind GeoLite2-City MMDB (graceful no-op if absent)
- Asset TTL cache: HashMap + Instant, 5-min TTL, backend wired in Phase 2
- Pipeline: parse -> enrich -> dedup -> storage write -> enriched publish
- Bus consumer loop with per-tenant topic subscription and nack -> DLQ
- Prometheus metrics: normalized events by format, GeoIP, asset cache,
  storage errors, pipeline latency histogram, consumer lag gauge
- Extended NormalizerConfig: raw_tenant_ids, consumer_group_id, metrics_addr
- Added maxminddb = "0.24" to workspace (ADR-017)
- ADR-018: simple HashMap cache, no external crate

Closes Phase 1.6 checklist in PHASES.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: 서버 안정성 개선 — 타임아웃/세션/메모리 방어 + E2E 테스트 20개 추가

백엔드/AI 수정 (6개 파일):
- _summary.py: DB 세션 inline 3곳 → 헬퍼 함수 통합, RAG 매칭 시 조기 반환
- _common.py: get_event_loop() deprecated 해소, contextvars로 모델명 요청별 격리
- _qa.py: document_content truncation을 truncate_by_paragraph로 변경
- _entry.py: if False 규정 검증 죽은 코드 80줄 정리 (기능 변화 없음)
- qdrant_store.py: list_documents에 max_docs=50 제한 (무제한 scroll 방지)
- chat.py: compound sub_query 60초 타임아웃, vLLM 스트리밍 chunk 30초 타임아웃,
  DB 저장 시 sources.content 200자 truncate

E2E 테스트 추가:
- intent-doc-compat.spec.js: intent↔문서Agent 호환성 13개 테스트
- doc-action-buttons.spec.js: 응답 하단 액션 버튼 7개 테스트

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: 3D human bones quiz — fixes and polish (#40)

* fix: cursor mutation ESLint errors and add bones3d tests

- Fix react-hooks/immutability errors in Anatomy3DRenderer and SkeletonSpike
  by storing gl.domElement in a ref rather than mutating the hook return value
- Add 32 unit tests for buildBone3DElements (region/type filtering, bilateral
  grouping, numbered grouping) and bone3DLocateDistance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: infinite re-render loop and canvas sizing in Anatomy3DRenderer

- Merge onModelBounds + onInitialView into single onModelReady callback
  in SkeletonMeshes to break dependency cycle (handleInitialView captured
  boneCenters from state, which changed on every render)
- Fix canvas not filling visualization area: use position:absolute/inset:0
  instead of height:100% (percentage height doesn't work in flex context
  without explicit parent height)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: toNodeKey must replace spaces with underscores to match Three.js sanitization

Three.js GLTFLoader sanitizes node names (spaces → underscores, dots stripped).
The old toNodeKey only stripped dots, so camera preset bone lookups (which use
original GLB names with spaces) couldn't find any meshes in the scene.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: use original GLB bone color (#e4ccb1) as default state

The previous gold tone (#c8a96e) looked unnatural. The new cream color
matches the GLB asset's original material base color.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move label mode to sidebar and respect element state visibility

- Remove labelMode selectToggle from quiz definition (no longer on setup page)
- Add local labelMode state with Off/Hover/On segmented control in sidebar
- Labels now respect element-states.md: answered states (correct, missed, etc.)
  always show labels; default/highlighted only show on hover regardless of mode.
  This prevents "On" mode from revealing unanswered bone names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: label visibility — only answered states, controlled by mode

Labels never show for default/highlighted/hidden (would reveal answers).
For answered states: 'on' shows all, 'hover' shows only hovered, 'off' hides all.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add anatomy-3d-renderer.md

Covers GLB mirroring, Three.js name sanitization, sidebar controls,
label visibility rules, element state color mapping, drag detection,
and camera animation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: grouped mesh mapping, z-fighting fix, layout fixes for 3D bones quiz

- Replace single meshName/side/directMesh on Anatomy3DElement with
  meshEntries array so grouped elements (bilateral/numbered) map ALL
  constituent meshes to the same quiz element
- Fix z-fighting: hide meshes in mirrored scene that already exist in
  original scene (midline, direct-mesh); keep non-quiz context bones
  visible for symmetry
- Add position: relative to visualization area in identify, ordered-recall,
  and prompted-recall modes so 3D renderer doesn't overflow its container
- Add meshEntries merge tests for bilateral and numbered grouping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: answer matching, camera jump, display labels, and sesamoid defaults

- Always run element label injection in ActiveQuiz (was skipped when no
  filters active, breaking free recall for grouped bones like "Femur")
- Add name_alternates column to bones CSV with common English names
  (kneecap, collarbone, jawbone, shinbone, etc.)
- Use element label (group name) for lastMatchedAnswer display in all
  three recall modes instead of raw CSV row name
- Hide 3D canvas until model is ready to prevent camera jump from
  initial [0,0,3] position
- Enable sesamoids by default in quiz registry and element builder
- Fix pre-existing useLocateQuiz test (string → options object)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Angus Johnson <angusj@canva.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refine JIRA search to Code Review transitions, filter build(deps) commits

- JIRA: only fetch tickets that moved FROM "Code Review" TO forward
  statuses (QA, Release Pending, Closed, Done, Resolved) during the
  commit date range, instead of all resolved tickets
- Filter out build(deps): bump commits from diff
- Make SHA range (commit comparison link) bolder and more visible
- Bump cache key to v7 to force re-processing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix codename detection crash on systems missing VERSION_CODENAME

regex_search with capture groups throws an exception (not None) when
there is no match in some Ansible versions. Switch to regex_findall,
which returns an empty list on no match, allowing or-chaining to work
correctly. Fixes the failure on Linux Mint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: region-split recognition - crop image into article + options

Instead of 3-pass feedback loop on full image, physically crop the
image at 50% height into article region (top) and options region
(bottom), then recognize each separately with targeted prompts.

Benefits:
- Each region gets model's full attention (no distraction)
- Higher effective resolution per region
- Only 2 API calls instead of 3 (faster)
- Targeted prompts for article vs MCQ options

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
